newsSinceJun6: Corning signed a multiyear, multibillion-dollar optical fiber agreement with Amazon (~Jun 8) to supply US AI data centers - its third hyperscaler megadeal of 2026 after Meta and Nvidia - sending shares +5.6% on Jun 8. Today's -9.35% (to ~$170) found no new fundamental negative: it reads as flow-driven profit-taking / AI-trade unwind on a ~95x TTM P/E name that already fell 10.1% on Jun 5, amid valuation downgrades (JPMorgan to neutral, Zacks to hold) and >$33M of insider selling over 90 days. Q2 guidance carries a ~$30M solar-shutdown cost headwind, but the AI fiber bottleneck thesis is intact and arguably strengthened.

sources.label: Business News Today - Corning jumps as Amazon locks in AI data center fiber
Investing.com - JPMorgan downgrades Corning on valuation
ts2.tech - Corning wins Amazon AI fiber deal; next hurdle

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new evidence since Jun 6 (third hyperscaler megadeal, Amazon) plus a -9.35% flow-driven flush with the thesis intact improves residual asymmetry at an already crowding-discounted rank 49; the ~17% give-back from recent highs de-crowds the trade somewhat.
verdicts.bull: Amazon joins Meta and Nvidia as locked-in multiyear fiber buyers, validating Corning as a contracted AI-connectivity bottleneck; an 18% core-sales, 30% core-EPS Q1 with mid-to-high-teens growth guided makes today's flush an asymmetry reset rather than a thesis break.
verdicts.neutral: Still up 87.5% YTD with a $161B cap and ~95x TTM P/E; the deal is real but the optical thesis is consensus, so the residual-alpha case rests on entry price, not discovery.
verdicts.bear: Three >9% daily swings in a week signal a momentum-crowded shareholder base unwinding; heavy insider selling, valuation downgrades and the solar-shutdown cost drag could compound if the AI-infra trade keeps de-rating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Further hyperscaler fiber contracts or capacity expansions converting the Amazon/Meta/Nvidia pipeline into raised full-year guidance toward the $20B run-rate target.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued AI-trade de-rating compressing a ~95x multiple regardless of execution; crowded fast-money ownership amplifies downside.

verdicts.rankRationale: This page already ranks GLW near the bottom precisely because the optical thesis went mainstream; the Amazon megadeal makes it MORE consensus, not more latent, so the latency case is unchanged - the flow-driven sell-off only marginally improves entry.
verdicts.bull: If today's flush extends, a contracted triple-hyperscaler fiber supplier at a materially lower multiple could re-enter the asymmetric zone; network centrality in AI connectivity is now contractually proven.
verdicts.neutral: Strategically strong, fully discovered: low conviction at rank 99 correctly reflects a name that is core infrastructure but no longer an early node - new deal flow confirms rather than changes that.
verdicts.bear: A 270% one-year run with insider selling and valuation downgrades means the latency premium is fully spent; further hyperscaler wins are already the consensus expectation embedded in the price.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A new under-the-radar segment (e.g., specialty glass or co-packaged-optics adjacency) emerging as a second, unpriced AI lever.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dead-money risk on this list: fully mainstream story where even good news no longer moves the latency case.
